PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-27-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Ryan Knutson, Esq., for and with Marianna Kjobmand, Petitioner.

The court confirms that there are no appearances to object to the instant petition. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration With Will Annexed. The lost will dated Jun-25-2020 is admitted to probate. Marianna Kjobmand is appointed as the Administrator With Will Annexed with full authority. Bond is not required. Amy Harrington is appointed probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Mar-11-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jul-26-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ryan Knutson appearing via zoom for and with Marianne Kjobmand (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding the relationship of Marianne Kjobmand and Wesley Cherry, that they lived together for 45 years, also that they believed to be in a domestic partnership but that there was no such thing for couples under the age of 62 in 2005, and that she would want to have the relationship recognized to avoid reassessment fees to the property she lives in now. Domestic partner property petition and Petition for lost will are taken under submission.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Wan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Continued To Jan-25-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ryan Knutson, via Zoom, for and with Marianne Kjobmand, Petitioner.

The court advises counsel that it has reviewed the papers submitted and its belief is that the petitioner needs to file a petition for probate of lost will and allows counsel to testify as to the court's position. Counsel reports that his client had possession of the will and offers advocacy on behalf of the instant petition. The court and counsel discuss the peculiar facts of the family tree and of a possible issue of notice. Counsel advises that he intends to do additional research regarding the family tree issue. The court advises that it would like him to submit a graphic family tree for clarification. The court suggests that counsel look at CA family code section 297 and that counsel should file a Petition for Lost Will in this case number and to note the special requirements for that specific petition specifically regarding providing notice.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Spousal Property is continued to Jan-25-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The hearing on the Petition to Admit Lost Will is to be set for the same date. Counsel is instructed to add that date on his petition and to indicate that the judge has ordered the petition calendared on that date. A Status of Filing Petition to Admit Lost Will is added for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
